title: TierProviderPolicy + buildChildConfig tier overlay
description: Add a closed difficulty-tier enum on FleetChild and a TierProviderPolicy that maps each tier to a planner/generator/evaluator provider block, overlaid in buildChildConfig BEFORE the existing shallow-merge so the result is byte-identical when tier is absent. New file src/fleet/tier-policy.ts exports: type RoleProviderBlock = {provider: ProviderName; model: string; endpoint?: string|null}; type TieredRoleBlock = {planner: RoleProviderBlock; generator: RoleProviderBlock; evaluator: RoleProviderBlock}; type DifficultyTier = 'default'|'cheap'|'standard'|'hard'|'frontier'; a TIER_POLICY table (cheap->DeepSeek openai-compat api.deepseek.com, standard->Grok openai-compat api.x.ai/v1, hard->Anthropic Sonnet, frontier->Anthropic Opus); resolveTier(tier): TieredRoleBlock|undefined ('default'/undefined -> undefined = NO overlay); knownTiers(). claude-code is NEVER in the table (children build = TOOL_ROLES). src/fleet/manifest.ts FleetChildSchema gains `tier: z.enum(['default','cheap','standard','hard','frontier']).optional()`. src/fleet/child-config.ts buildChildConfig adds an applyTier(base, child.tier) step that, when resolveTier returns a block, sets base.planner/generator/evaluator = {...base.role, ...block.role} — applied BEFORE the UNCHANGED `const merged = {...base, ...(child.config ?? {})}` at child-config.ts:43 so an explicit child.config still wins. When tier is undefined or 'default', applyTier returns base UNCHANGED and the whole function output is byte-identical to today.

successCriteria.description: The project builds with zero TypeScript errors (npm run build) and zero typecheck errors in strict mode (npm run typecheck).
The full test suite passes with zero new failures (npm run test); only the 6 known cockpit-integration MCP failures may fail; the existing fleet child-config/manifest tests pass unchanged.

FleetChildSchema parses a child WITH tier (any enum value) and WITHOUT tier; a tier outside the closed enum is a ZodError; a manifest with no tier on any child parses field-for-field identically to before (the new field is optional and absent).

TierProviderPolicy.resolveTier returns undefined for 'default' and undefined input, and the correct TieredRoleBlock for cheap (DeepSeek), standard (Grok openai-compat https://api.x.ai/v1), hard (anthropic Sonnet), frontier (anthropic Opus). No tier block places claude-code on any role.

buildChildConfig(child) with NO tier returns a BoberConfig deep-equal to the pre-change DeepSeek-default output — proven by a test that constructs the expected config (planner/generator/evaluator on DeepSeek) and asserts deepEqual. This is the byte-identical guarantee.

buildChildConfig(child) with tier='standard' yields planner/generator/evaluator on provider 'openai-compat' with endpoint 'https://api.x.ai/v1'; tier='hard' yields provider 'anthropic' + a Sonnet model; tier='frontier' yields 'anthropic' + an Opus model; tier='cheap' yields DeepSeek. Each asserted on the parsed BoberConfig.

child.config precedence is preserved: a child with BOTH tier='standard' AND child.config.generator={provider:'anthropic',model:'...'} yields generator on anthropic (child.config wins over the tier block), proving the overlay runs BEFORE the shallow-merge at child-config.ts:43.
lint passes (npm run lint, zero errors); the change is confined to tier-policy.ts (new), manifest.ts, child-config.ts (+ tests); no new SDK/network imports; ProviderName unchanged.

nonGoals: Do NOT place claude-code in any tier block (children build = tool roles).
Do NOT add the ToolRoleGuard (Sprint 3) — though tier blocks must never produce a claude-code tool role in the first place.
Do NOT change the decomposer EXPAND prompt or the never-throw validateManifest shape guard.
Do NOT add BoberConfig.fleet / blackboard / Phase B fields.
Do NOT alter the `merged = {...base, ...(child.config ?? {})}` line itself (apply the overlay BEFORE it).
stopConditions: A tier-less child config is deep-equal to today's DeepSeek default, each tier maps to its specified provider block, child.config still wins, and build/typecheck/test/lint are green with no new failures
definitionOfDone: src/fleet/tier-policy.ts exports DifficultyTier, RoleProviderBlock, TieredRoleBlock, TierProviderPolicy (resolveTier/knownTiers), and a TIER_POLICY table (cheap=DeepSeek, standard=Grok api.x.ai, hard=anthropic Sonnet, frontier=anthropic Opus; default=>undefined). src/fleet/manifest.ts FleetChildSchema has optional tier enum. src/fleet/child-config.ts applies the tier overlay before the unchanged shallow-merge, byte-identical when tier absent. Collocated tests cover schema parse (with/without/invalid tier), resolveTier per tier, the deep-equal byte-identical no-tier config, each tier's resolved provider block, and child.config precedence. Build/typecheck/test/lint green.
assumptions: buildChildConfig today (child-config.ts:20-44) sets planner/generator/evaluator to DEEPSEEK_* then `const merged = {...base, ...(child.config ?? {})}` then BoberConfigSchema.parse(merged); the tier overlay is inserted on `base` BEFORE that merge line so child.config precedence is preserved.

RoleProviderBlock matches the per-role section shape buildChildConfig already writes (provider, model, endpoint); anthropic tiers set endpoint to null/undefined, openai-compat tiers set the deepseek/x.ai endpoint.

Tier model ids use model-resolver shorthands or full ids (deepseek -> deepseek + api.deepseek.com; grok -> grok + api.x.ai from Sprint 1; hard -> a Sonnet id e.g. 'sonnet'/'claude-sonnet-4-6'; frontier -> an Opus id e.g. 'opus'/'claude-opus-4-8'); ids are overridable via child.config.

Grok routing/endpoint from Sprint 1 (resolveProviderModel grok -> openai-compat + api.x.ai/v1) is available, so the standard tier block can specify provider 'openai-compat' + that endpoint + a grok model.
generatorNotes:
Read FIRST: src/fleet/child-config.ts (FULL — the DeepSeek hard-set at :24-41 and the `const merged = {...base, ...(child.config ?? {})}` at :43, then BoberConfigSchema.parse(merged) at :44); src/fleet/manifest.ts (FleetChildSchema :6-9, FleetManifestSchema :13-17); src/config/schema.ts (the per-role section shape: provider/model/endpoint fields, so RoleProviderBlock matches); src/providers/factory.ts ProviderName; arch ADR-2 in .bober/architecture/arch-20260618-heterogeneous-multi-provider-agent-team-adr-2.md.

New src/fleet/tier-policy.ts: define DifficultyTier union; RoleProviderBlock {provider: ProviderName; model: string; endpoint?: string | null}; TieredRoleBlock {planner,generator,evaluator: RoleProviderBlock}; a const TIER_POLICY: Record<Exclude<DifficultyTier,'default'>, TieredRoleBlock> with the four entries (cheap: all three roles -> {provider:'openai-compat', model:'deepseek', endpoint:'https://api.deepseek.com'}; standard -> {provider:'openai-compat', model:'grok', endpoint:'https://api.x.ai/v1'}; hard -> {provider:'anthropic', model:'sonnet', endpoint: null}; frontier -> {provider:'anthropic', model:'opus', endpoint: null}); export const tierPolicy: TierProviderPolicy with resolveTier(tier) => (tier && tier !== 'default') ? TIER_POLICY[tier] : undefined, and knownTiers().

manifest.ts: add `tier: z.enum(['default','cheap','standard','hard','frontier']).optional()` to FleetChildSchema. Keep config field.

child-config.ts: after the three DeepSeek hard-sets and BEFORE `const merged = ...`, insert: `const block = tierPolicy.resolveTier(child.tier); if (block) { base.planner = {...base.planner, ...block.planner}; base.generator = {...base.generator, ...block.generator}; base.evaluator = {...base.evaluator, ...block.evaluator}; }`. Then the UNCHANGED merge + parse. When block is undefined the function is byte-identical. Be careful endpoint: for anthropic tiers set endpoint to null/undefined exactly as the existing anthropic config sections do (check schema default) so the parsed config matches a normal anthropic role section.

Tests: tier-policy.test.ts (resolveTier per tier incl default/undefined => undefined; assert no block has provider 'claude-code'); manifest.test.ts additions (parse with/without/invalid tier); child-config.test.ts additions (deep-equal no-tier vs an explicit expected DeepSeek config = the byte-identical proof; each tier's resolved planner/generator/evaluator provider+endpoint+model; child.config-wins-over-tier precedence). Use BoberConfigSchema to build the expected config in the deep-equal test so field defaults match. Follow ESM .js imports, import type, unicode section headers.
evaluatorNotes: sc-2-3: parse a FleetManifest with a child tier of each enum value, one with no tier, and one with tier:'bogus' (expect ZodError); assert the no-tier parse equals the prior shape. sc-2-4: resolveTier('default')===undefined, resolveTier(undefined)===undefined, resolveTier('standard').generator.endpoint==='https://api.x.ai/v1', resolveTier('hard').generator.provider==='anthropic', resolveTier('frontier') Opus; grep tier-policy.ts to confirm no 'claude-code' literal. sc-2-5 (CRITICAL byte-identical): build the expected DeepSeek-default BoberConfig and assert deepEqual to buildChildConfig({folder,task}) with no tier. sc-2-6: buildChildConfig with each tier -> assert planner/generator/evaluator provider+endpoint+model. sc-2-7: child with tier:'standard' + config.generator={provider:'anthropic',model:'sonnet'} -> assert resulting generator.provider==='anthropic' (child.config wins). sc-2-2: FULL suite, only the 6 known cockpit failures allowed. Build/typecheck/lint clean.
